issues.title: Incorrect revenue calculation
issues.problem: The query is summing all transaction amounts without filtering by status 'COMPLETED', which violates the business rule.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as lifetime_value

issues.title: Missing comments on complex logic
issues.problem: The HAVING clause lacks comments explaining the business logic behind the threshold.
issues.fix:
-- Only include customers with lifetime value greater than 1000
HAVING SUM(t.amount) > 1000

issues.title: Inconsistent naming convention
issues.problem: The alias 'lifetime_value' could be more descriptive, such as 'total_completed_value'.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as total_completed_value

review.corrected_sql:
SELECT c.customer_name, c.email, c.tier,
       SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as total_completed_value,
       COUNT(t.transaction_id) as total_orders
FROM dim_customer c
LEFT JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id
WHERE t.status = 'COMPLETED'
GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.email
-- Only include customers with lifetime value greater than 1000
HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) > 1000
ORDER BY total_completed_value DESC;
review.summary: The query had a critical correctness issue with revenue calculation and minor readability issues. The corrected query now properly calculates lifetime value and includes comments for clarity.

issues.title: Incorrect calculation of failure rate
issues.problem: The failure rate is being calculated incorrectly by dividing the failed_count by total_count within the same row, which is incorrect.

issues.title: Correlated subquery
issues.line: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fact_transactions f2 WHERE f2.status = 'FAILED' AND f2.transaction_date = t.transaction_date
issues.problem: The correlated subquery can be inefficient as it runs for each row in the outer query.
issues.fix: Use a JOIN to avoid correlated subquery.

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic, especially around the calculation of failure rate.
issues.fix: Add comments to explain the logic.

review.corrected_sql:
WITH failed_transactions AS (
  SELECT transaction_date, COUNT(*) as failed_count
  FROM fact_transactions
  WHERE status = 'FAILED'
  GROUP BY transaction_date
),
total_transactions AS (
  SELECT transaction_date, COUNT(*) as total_count
  FROM fact_transactions
  GROUP BY transaction_date
)
SELECT t.transaction_date,
       f.failed_count,
       t.total_count,
       (f.failed_count::DECIMAL / t.total_count) * 100 as failure_rate
FROM total_transactions t
LEFT JOIN failed_transactions f ON t.transaction_date = f.transaction_date
ORDER BY t.transaction_date;
review.summary: The query had a critical correctness issue with the failure rate calculation and a performance issue with the correlated subquery. Readability was also improved by adding comments.

issues.title: Incorrect revenue calculation
issues.problem: The query sums all transaction amounts without filtering for 'COMPLETED' status only.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) AS total_spend

issues.title: Incorrect failure rate calculation
issues.problem: The query does not calculate the failure rate.
issues.fix: COUNT(CASE WHEN t.status = 'FAILED' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) AS failure_rate

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic and calculations.
issues.fix: Add comments to explain the purpose of each part of the query.

issues.title: Inconsistent naming
issues.problem: The alias 'total_spend' could be more descriptive, such as 'total_completed_spend'.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) AS total_completed_spend

review.corrected_sql: SELECT c.customer_name, c.email, SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) AS total_completed_spend, COUNT(*) AS transaction_count, COUNT(CASE WHEN t.status = 'FAILED' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) AS failure_rate FROM dim_customer c JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id WHERE t.status IN ('COMPLETED', 'FAILED') GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.email ORDER BY total_completed_spend DESC;
review.summary: The query had critical correctness issues with revenue calculation and missing failure rate calculation. Readability was also improved by adding comments and more descriptive aliases.

issues.title: Missing filter for completed transactions
issues.problem: The query does not filter transactions by status 'COMPLETED' to calculate revenue.

issues.title: Implicit join syntax
issues.problem: Using implicit join syntax can lead to less readable and potentially less performant queries.
issues.fix: FROM fact_transactions t JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the purpose and logic.
issues.fix: Add comments to explain the purpose of each clause.

review.corrected_sql: SELECT m.merchant_name, SUM(t.amount) as total_revenue, COUNT(*) as txn_count FROM fact_transactions t JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id WHERE t.status = 'COMPLETED' AND t.transaction_date > '2024-01-01' GROUP BY m.merchant_name ORDER BY total_revenue LIMIT 10;
review.summary: The query needs a filter for completed transactions to correctly calculate revenue and should use explicit join syntax for better readability and potential performance benefits.
